raw_consumer_statement:
My complaint is with Barclays US, the credit card company that I used to charge airplane tickets from the agency, XXXX XXXX, online. I purchased airline tickets for my husband and myself online through XXXX using my Barclays credit card on XX/XX/XXXX for charges totaling {$2600.00}. The so-called price reduction was based on a free 15-day trial membership basis which I agreed to, but also cancelled within the proper time frame. Less than 24 hours later, on XX/XX/XXXX, I cancelled my booking and received an email confirming that my refund was being processed.

In reviewing my transaction history for my Barclays Credit card account, I discovered that Id only received a partial credit of {$2100.00} and not the full credit of {$2600.00}. I disputed this immediately, on XX/XX/XXXX and received the full credit on my XXXX statement which I thought resolved the issue. 

Instead of the matter being closed I was recharged the {$530.00} on my XXXX statement by Barclays, and after the back and forth, speaking to their representatives, because I have been unable to contact XXXX, and although I sent the email confirmation of the full refund demanding a reinvestigation, and demanded to know what proof they had that the charge was legitimate on XX/XX/XXXX, I was informed that they were unable to pursue the matter and that the {$530.00} balance remained due. 

Barclays is attempting to collect money for XXXX  services/ goods never delivered and I have provided proof of such. This continued attempt to collect is intentional, harmful to my credit and peace of mind, and in my view criminal. This is unacceptable and why I am contacting this department to file a complaint.
